records.ety: [L. arbitrarius, fr. arbiter: cf. F. arbitraire. See Arbiter.]

defs: Depending on will or discretion; not governed by any fixed rules; as, an arbitrary decision; an arbitrary punishment.
quotes: It was wholly arbitrary in them to do so.
Rank pretends to fix the value of every one, and is the most arbitrary of all things.

defs: Exercised according to one's own will or caprice, and therefore conveying a notion of a tendency to abuse the possession of power.
quotes: Arbitrary power is most easily established on the ruins of liberty abused licentiousness.

defs: Despotic; absolute in power; bound by no law; harsh and unforbearing; tyrannical; as, an arbitrary prince or government.
